name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
description: A central sector scheme that provides direct financial assistance to landholding farmer families across India to help them buy agricultural inputs and meet household needs.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year, paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 directly into the bank accounts of beneficiaries every four months.

eligibility.details: All landholding farmer families who own cultivable land in their names. Excludes institutional landholders and professional taxpayers.

application_process: Farmers can self-register via the PM-KISAN portal (pmkisan.gov.in) using their Aadhaar card, land records (Khatauni), and bank details, or apply through the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC).

name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
description: Government-sponsored crop insurance scheme that integrates multiple stakeholders to protect farmers from crop loss due to non-preventable natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
benefits: Comprehensive insurance cover against yield losses, localized calamities, and post-harvest losses. Farmers pay very low premium rates: 1.5% for Rabi crops, 2.0% for Kharif crops, and 5.0% for commercial/horticultural crops, with the balance subsidized by the government.

eligibility.details: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers, are eligible. Highly recommended for areas prone to erratic monsoon, drought, or floods.

application_process: Eligible farmers can apply online through the PMFBY portal (pmfby.gov.in), through cooperative banks, commercial banks, or via insurance agents before the crop-sowing deadline for the season.

name: PMKSY - Per Drop More Crop (Micro Irrigation Subsidy)
description: A national program promoting water-use efficiency through modern micro-irrigation technologies like Drip and Sprinkler systems, ensuring 'Per Drop More Crop'.
benefits: Financial assistance (subsidies) on the installation cost of drip and sprinkler irrigation systems. Small and marginal farmers receive a 55% subsidy, while other categories of farmers receive 45%. Certain states top this up to 80-90%.

eligibility.details: Farmers owning cultivable land who have access to a permanent water source are eligible. Priority is given to small and marginal farmers (holding less than 5 acres).

application_process: Apply online through the respective state's Horticulture or Agriculture Department portal (e.g., dbt.mpdage.org for MP, or Mahadbt for Maharashtra) or contact the local block horticulture officer.

name: Namo Shetkari Mahasanman Nidhi Yojna (Maharashtra)
description: Maharashtra government's additional direct income support scheme, designed to run in parallel with the central PM-KISAN scheme.
benefits: An additional ₹6,000 per year, paid in three installments of ₹2,000, bringing the total direct financial benefit to Maharashtra farmers to ₹12,000 per year when combined with PM-KISAN.

eligibility.details: Active landholding farmers in Maharashtra who are already registered and approved as beneficiaries under the central PM-KISAN scheme.

application_process: No separate registration is required. Beneficiaries are automatically identified and mapped from the approved PM-KISAN database of Maharashtra.

name: YSR Rythu Bharosa (Andhra Pradesh)
description: A comprehensive welfare scheme by the Government of Andhra Pradesh providing substantial financial assistance to agricultural families.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹13,500 per year (including ₹6,000 from central PM-KISAN) for five years to meet seed, fertilizer, and operational costs.

eligibility.details: Landowner families in AP. Additionally, tenant farmers belonging to SC, ST, BC, and Minority communities are also fully eligible for the state-funded portion of the benefit.

application_process: Registration is carried out through village secretariats, local Rythu Bharosa Kendras (RBKs), or through physical verification by village agricultural assistants.

name: UP Solar Pump Yojana (Kusum Yojana - Component B)
description: Uttar Pradesh government scheme subsidizing solar-powered water pumps for off-grid irrigation, reducing dependency on costly diesel and unreliable electricity.
benefits: Up to 60% subsidy on the purchase and installation of solar pumps ranging from 2 HP to 10 HP (DC and AC surface/submersible models).

eligibility.details: Farmers residing and owning cultivable land in Uttar Pradesh with pre-existing borewells or shallow wells suitable for solar pump installations.

application_process: Interested farmers must register online on the UP Agriculture Department portal (upagriculture.com) and book solar pumps when tokens are released, by paying a demand draft of the farmer's share.

name: Punjab Crop Residue Management (CRM) Scheme
description: Punjab government's environmental and agricultural scheme providing heavy subsidies on crop residue management machines to curb stubble burning after paddy harvests.
benefits: 50% subsidy on the purchase of CRM machinery (such as Happy Seeder, Smart Seeder, Paddy Straw Chopper, Mulcher, Baler, and Rotavator) for individual farmers, and up to 80% subsidy for Cooperative Societies and Farmers Producer Organizations (FPOs) setting up Custom Hiring Centres.

eligibility.details: Farmers in Punjab, especially those engaged in rice-wheat crop rotations which generate heavy straw residue.

application_process: Apply online through the Punjab Agriculture Department portal (agrimachinerypb.com) during the announced application window, usually before the Kharif harvesting season.

name: Saat Pagla Kalyanna - Tractor & Machinery Subsidy (Gujarat)
description: Gujarat state scheme aimed at promoting farm mechanization among small and marginal farmers to improve agricultural efficiency.
benefits: Subsidies up to ₹45,000 to ₹60,000 on the purchase of small tractors (less than 20 HP) and varying subsidies on power tillers, smart hand-tools, crop protection equipment, and temporary storage sheds.

eligibility.details: Small and marginal farmers registered in Gujarat holding a valid 8-A land record.

application_process: Submit online applications via the Gujarat i-Khedut portal (ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in) and submit printouts with land documents to the local taluka extension officer.

name: Rajasthan Solar Pump & Drip Irrigation Scheme
description: Rajasthan government's solar pump subsidy scheme combined with micro-irrigation to conserve water in arid and semi-arid regions.
benefits: Up to 60% subsidy for general category farmers and up to 75% subsidy for small, marginal, SC, and ST farmers for installing solar water pumps, plus 70% subsidy on drip/sprinkler systems.

eligibility.details: Farmers in Rajasthan owning cultivable agricultural land with at least 0.5 hectares of land and a micro-irrigation system installed or planned.

application_process: Apply online via the Raj Kisan Saathi portal (rajkisan.rajasthan.gov.in) using Jan Aadhaar card credentials.

name: Mukhyamantri Kisan Kalyan Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
description: Madhya Pradesh state welfare scheme providing supplemental direct income support to farmers to offset farming expenses.
benefits: An additional ₹4,000 per year (paid in two equal installments of ₹2,000) on top of the central PM-KISAN, bringing total direct support to ₹10,000 per year.

eligibility.details: Landowning farmers in Madhya Pradesh who are approved beneficiaries under the PM-KISAN scheme.

application_process: Linked automatically to PM-KISAN. Farmers register through their local Patwari or online at the MP Sara portal (sara.mp.gov.in).

name: Kalaignarin All Village Integrated Agricultural Development Programme (Tamil Nadu)
description: Tamil Nadu government's comprehensive village welfare scheme targeting water conservation and agricultural input support.
benefits: Distribution of agricultural tool kits (spade, crowbar, sickle), 50% subsidy on high-yield seeds and organic fertilizers, and full subsidies for digging farm ponds and installing solar fencing.

eligibility.details: Preference is given to small and marginal farmers (under 5 acres) residing in notified village panchayats in Tamil Nadu.

application_process: Register via the Tamil Nadu Uzhavan App or apply in writing to the local Assistant Director of Agriculture at the block level.

name: Krishi Bhagya Scheme (Karnataka)
description: Karnataka government scheme aimed at dryland agriculture, focusing on rainwater harvesting and conservation technologies.
benefits: Subsidies ranging from 80% to 90% (for SC/ST) for constructing farm ponds (Krishi Honda), purchasing diesel water lifting pumps, and installing protective shade nets and drip irrigation.

eligibility.details: Farmers practicing dryland agriculture in rainfed blocks of Karnataka. The farm must have space to dig a farm pond.

application_process: Apply via the Karnataka State Kaveri/Fruts portal or submit applications directly to the Raitha Samparka Kendra (RSK) in the local block.
